perturbation.original_text: Data in the Michigan Trauma Registry will be dis closed to Regional Trauma Networks through access to the Michigan Trauma Registry database. Individuals gra nted access to the Michigan Trauma Registry database will be required to execute user agreements with MDHHS .
perturbation.changed_text: Data in the Michigan Trauma Registry will be shared with Regional Trauma Networks through access to the Michigan Trauma Registry database. Individuals granted access to the Michigan Trauma Registry database will be encouraged to execute user agreements with MDHHS .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosed' to 'shared' and 'required' to 'encouraged' weakens the mandatory nature of data protection and user agreements. This contradicts the overall purpose of the agreement, which is to protect data. It particularly contradicts paragraph 4 which states that MDHHS will use appropriate safeguards.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use appropriate safeguards to prevent the use or disclosure of the information other than as provided by this A greement;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'disclosed' to 'shared' and 'required' to 'encouraged' weakens the obligation to protect data, creating a conflict with the requirement to use 'appropriate safeguards to prevent the use or disclosure of the information other than as provided by this Agreement.' The modified text introduces a strong contradiction by suggesting that user agreements are now optional, undermining the safeguards mentioned in the contradicted text. This leads to a clear and direct conflict regarding the mandatory nature of data protection.

perturbation.original_text: With regard to the data disclosed to MDHHS under this Agreement, the MDHHS agrees to: 1. Use and disclose the data only in accordance with this A greement, or as otherwise authorized by law;
perturbation.changed_text: With regard to the data provided to MDHHS under this Agreement, the MDHHS agrees to: 1. Utilize the data in accordance with general practices, or as deemed fit by internal counsel;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosed' to 'provided' and 'Use and disclose the data only in accordance with this Agreement, or as otherwise authorized by law' to 'Utilize the data in accordance with general practices, or as deemed fit by internal counsel' gives MDHHS broad discretion on how the data is used, contradicting the core principle of the agreement and the explicit purpose mentioned earlier in the text. This creates an in-text contradiction and potential conflict with established laws regarding the disclosure of information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The data provided to MDHHS will be used only for purposes of the Michigan Trauma Registry, consistent with Part 209 of the Public Health Code and the Michigan Administrative Code R 325.125 through 325.138.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the data usage terms. The original text mandates data use 'only in accordance with this Agreement, or as otherwise authorized by law.' The changed text allows MDHHS to 'Utilize the data in accordance with general practices, or as deemed fit by internal counsel.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which specifies that the data 'will be used only for purposes of the Michigan Trauma Registry.' The changed text introduces broad discretion, conflicting with the explicitly defined purpose in the contradicted text, therefore satisfying the condition for a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Report to the Reporting Entity within ten (10) business days of discovery any actual or suspected inappropriate use, disclosure , or breach of information from the Michigan Trauma Registry that involves data submitted under this Agreement ;
perturbation.changed_text: Inform the Data Provider within a reasonable timeframe of any potential misuse of data originating from the Michigan Trauma Registry;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Report to the Reporting Entity within ten (10) business days of discovery any actual or suspected inappropriate use, disclosure , or breach of information from the Michigan Trauma Registry that involves data submitted under this Agreement ;' to 'Inform the Data Provider within a reasonable timeframe of any potential misuse of data originating from the Michigan Trauma Registry;' weakens the obligation to report breaches, making it less specific and less timely. Substituting 'Reporting Entity' with 'Data Provider' (from the first perturbation) further complicates things. It contradicts the intent to maintain data security by creating vague responsibilities. The original text clearly mandates a 10-day window of reporting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Report to the Reporting Entity within ten (10) business days of discovery any actual or suspected inappropriate use, disclosure , or breach of information from the Michigan Trauma Registry that involves data submitted under this Agreement ;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific reporting timeframe (10 business days) with a vague 'reasonable timeframe' and the 'Reporting Entity' to the 'Data Provider'. Since the original text and the contradicted text are exactly the same, this introduces ambiguity and vagueness regarding when and to whom potential misuses of data need to be reported. The original text mandates a 10-day window of reporting, which the changed text negates. The change directly weakens the obligation and creates a clear conflict/inconsistency, which is a strong in-text contradiction, because it changes the deadline and recipient of the report.

perturbation.original_text: Destroy all originals and copies of potentially identifiable information , in any format , in accordance with industry standards when no longer needed. This includes, but is not limited to: magnetic tape s, micro di sk files, paper records, etc. ;
perturbation.changed_text: Discard originals and copies of sensitive data as deemed necessary, following internal disposal protocols, when retention is no longer mandated by organizational directives;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Destroy all originals and copies of potentially identifiable information , in any format , in accordance with industry standards when no longer needed. This includes, but is not limited to: magnetic tape s, micro di sk files, paper records, etc. ;' to 'Discard originals and copies of sensitive data as deemed necessary, following internal disposal protocols, when retention is no longer mandated by organizational directives;' introduces vagueness by not explicitly referencing industry standards and giving the data recipients more autonomy. This reduces the enforceability of appropriate data handling and directly impacts data security.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use appropriate safeguards to prevent the use or disclosure of the information other than as provided by this A greement;
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates destroying data per industry standards, while the changed text allows discarding data as 'deemed necessary' following 'internal disposal protocols'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which requires 'appropriate safeguards to prevent the use or disclosure of the information other than as provided by this A greement'. The changed text introduces vagueness and gives more autonomy to the data recipients, weakening data protection. This directly conflicts with the requirement for 'appropriate safeguards' as the change reduces the enforceability of appropriate data handling, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreeme nt may be terminated upon sixty days (60 ) written notice of the non - terminating party by the terminating party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Understanding may be dissolved with a two-month advance notification delivered in written form by the departing faction to the remaining faction.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the terminology from 'Agreement' to 'Understanding', 'terminated' to 'dissolved,' 'sixty days' to 'two-month,' 'non-terminating party' to 'remaining faction,' and 'terminating party' to 'departing faction,' uncertainty is introduced regarding the enforceability and legal weight of the obligations. The initial text suggests a legally binding contract with a clear termination clause, while the revised wording implies a more informal arrangement. This inconsistency affects the entire document and undermines the original intent of ensuring data protection. It generates uncertainty in enforcement by changing agreement to understanding which denotes nonbinding arrangement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties to this Agreement are the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services and _____________________________________________________________________________ (Reporting Entity ). This Agreement is effective when the authorized representatives of each party have signed it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Understanding.' The contradicted text specifically refers to 'this Agreement.' By changing the foundational terminology from a legally binding 'Agreement' to a more informal 'Understanding,' the document introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the section that defines the parties to 'this Agreement' as having signed a legally binding contract. The changed terminology introduces uncertainty about whether the document is legally enforceable, thus creating a strong contradiction.
